definition: Premium and Discount are the upper and lower halves of a price range, divided by the 50% Fibonacci level (Equilibrium). Price above 50% is premium (overvalued, favoring shorts), below is discount (undervalued, favoring longs). The range can be based on swing points, displacement legs, consolidation ranges, or opening prices.
identification_rules: Identify a significant price range (e.g., swing high to swing low, displacement leg, consolidation range, Asia/London range, or midnight/8:30 open levels).
Apply Fibonacci retracement from the range low to high (or vice versa); the 0.5 level is the equilibrium (EQ).
The area above the 0.5 level is premium; below is discount.
For bullish bias, use the most recent swing low to swing high; for bearish bias, swing high to swing low.
In premium, identify resistance structures (FVG, Order Block, Breaker Block, equal highs); in discount, identify support structures (FVG, Order Block, Breaker Block, equal lows).
Multiple structures overlapping in the same price area form a PD Array.
Cross-timeframe validation: identify PD Array on higher timeframe, then drill down to lower timeframe for additional confluence.
PD Array often lies within OTE zones (61.8%, 70.5%, 78.6% retracement). If OB open price is not in OTE, use its midpoint (50%) as alternative.
Include previous day/week highs/lows, swing highs/lows as components of PD Array.
When standard deviation projections (e.g., -2 to -2.5, -4) align with FVG or OB, the PD Array strength increases.
In Mitigation Block areas, map historical order blocks from left to right to identify stacked support/resistance.
Update premium/discount zones when price makes new highs or lows.
Apply to multiple timeframes: higher timeframe determines direction, lower timeframe for entry.
Use additional Fibonacci levels (0.62, 0.79) within premium/discount to refine optimal entry zones (OTE).
trading_implication: In discount, prioritize long setups at PD Array (FVG, OB, etc.); in premium, prioritize short setups. Price deeper into discount/premium offers better risk-reward; entries near equilibrium have ~1:1 RR. After market structure shift (MSS/CSD), price often retraces to premium/discount. Use PD Array as target: longs target premium, shorts target discount. Dynamic updates required. In trending markets, align with trend: buy discount in uptrend, sell premium in downtrend.
common_mistakes: Trading opposite direction: buying in premium or selling in discount.
Using incorrect swing points for Fibonacci, misidentifying zones.
Failing to update zones after new highs/lows, rendering zones obsolete.
Confusing multiple nested ranges; each range has its own equilibrium.
Treating premium/discount as fixed support/resistance rather than relative zones.
Relying solely on premium/discount without confluence from FVG, OB, market structure, etc.
Entering near equilibrium, ignoring poor 1:1 risk-reward.
Confusing external and internal liquidity, leading to wrong range selection.
Not aligning higher timeframe PD Array with lower timeframe structure.
Using PD Array without prior displacement/MSS/CSD confirmation.
Ignoring nested structures within PD Array, missing precise entries.
Considering already swept structures as still valid.
Expecting reversal after PD Array is fully swept, instead of trend continuation.
Confusing low-resistance and high-resistance liquidity, misjudging price penetration.
Using PD Array in consolidation instead of expansion phase.
Confusing Mitigation Block with Breaker Block.
Only using single open level (e.g., midnight) instead of multiple (midnight and 8:30) for deep premium/discount.

related_concepts.term: Change in the state of delivery

merge_notes: All four input cards are from ICT school; no school conflict. The first two cards (ICT) provide extensive detail on PD Array and OTE, while the third (ICT Updated) is a concise summary, and the fourth (SMC-Extra-Supplement) adds trend alignment nuance. Unified card preserves all identification rules and mistakes from the first two cards, plus trend-specific rules from the fourth. The third card's definition was used as base for brevity.
